compiled_state.title: As a DSO developer, I can register preplanning.interactive in the config infrastructure so the key is recognized by validation tooling and automation pipelines
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add the `preplanning.interactive` config key to the DSO config infrastructure ŌĆö including `dso-config.conf`, `validate-config.sh` KNOWN_KEYS, and `workflow-config-schema.json` ŌĆö and bump the config version to 1.1.0.

**Why**: Before preplanning can read the key at startup, the key must exist in the config file and be registered in the schema/validation layer. Without registering it in `validate-config.sh` KNOWN_KEYS, CI breaks on any project that sets the key.

**Scope**:
- IN: `.claude/dso-config.conf` (add `preplanning.interactive=false`, bump version 1.0.0 ŌåÆ 1.1.0); `plugins/dso/scripts/validate-config.sh` (add `preplanning.interactive` to KNOWN_KEYS array); `plugins/dso/docs/workflow-config-schema.json` (add `preplanning` object with `interactive` boolean property)
- OUT: Behavioral changes to preplanning SKILL.md (S2); human-readable documentation in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (S3); onboarding changes (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/dso-config.conf` contains `preplanning.interactive=false` and its version field is `1.1.0`
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: "This project's `.claude/dso-config.conf` sets `preplanning.interactive=false` explicitly"
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/scripts/validate-config.sh` lists `preplanning.interactive` in its KNOWN_KEYS array and `validate-config.sh` exits 0 when the key is present in a config file (no CI breakage)
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: "An automated preplanning invocation... completes end-to-end without pausing on any checkpoint"
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/workflow-config-schema.json` includes a `preplanning` object with an `interactive` boolean property so schema validation accepts the new key
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: "`CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md` and `workflow-config-schema.json` document `preplanning.interactive`"
- When this story is complete, unit tests cover the KNOWN_KEYS registration (validate-config exits 0 with the key present, exits non-zero with an unknown key) and the schema property addition
  ŌåÉ Satisfies: TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Configuration] `read-config.sh` returns empty string for absent keys ŌĆö implementation of the conditional check in S2 MUST use `[[ "$PREPLANNING_INTERACTIVE" == "true" ]]` not `!= "false"` to treat absent key as non-interactive
- [Reliability] Bumping dso-config.conf version from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0 signals a minor addition; review `version` field format matches what validate-config.sh expects

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue ŌĆö one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
